title: Smoky Stir-fry Yautia
description: A smoky, crispy solo-dinner dish featuring yautia roots that are friendly for pescatarians.

ingredients.name: Yautia (daikon)
ingredients.notes: Peel and dice into thin strips.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop into fine pieces for quick cooking.

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Dice thinly, leave seeds and membranes intact for added smokiness.

ingredients.name: Tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a mild or low-sodium variety to avoid excess salt.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: For the crispy texture and as a smoking medium if using a smoker.

instructions: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C) for roasting or use a gas grill set to high heat.
Slice yautia into thin strips. If not roasting, blanch the strips in boiling water for about 2 minutes before stir-frying.
In a small bowl, mix tomato sauce with olive oil and enough water to reach a pouring consistency.
In a skillet or wok, sauté chopped onion until translucent. Add minced garlic and diced red bell pepper, cook for an additional minute.
Add yautia strips to the skillet. Pour the tomato-oil mixture over the vegetables, ensuring all are well-coated with sauce.
If using a smoker, place the skillet on the grill or in your oven and let it smoke until the edges of the yautia turn crispy (about 10-15 minutes).
For those not smoking: Cook for another minute or so to ensure all vegetables are tender. Season with salt if needed.
Serve hot as a side dish or a main course.
